behaviors.description: A cell containing =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10
behaviors.justification: Formula parsing, range iteration, and numeric summation are entirely in-process logic with no external dependencies. The entire engine is a single object that can be tested in isolation. A mock would not hide any real failure because all logic is local and deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1 through A10 with known numeric values (1 through 10). Create a cell containing =SUM(A1:A10). Evaluate the formula and assert the result equals 55. Test partial ranges (=SUM(A1:A5) equals 15) and single cells (=SUM(A1:A1) equals 1). Test edge case where range contains zeros and negative numbers. Test that range parsing handles both absolute (A$1:$A$10) and relative references.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test fully captures the behavior; no module boundaries or external I/O involved.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula produces the correct cascaded result
behaviors.justification: Formula dependency resolution and cascaded evaluation are in-process algorithms. The engine's dependency graph and topological evaluation are entirely local and can be tested by exercising the engine end-to-end with dependent formulas and asserting intermediate and final values.
behaviors.test_description: Create cell A1 with value 5. Create cell B1 with formula =A1*2 (expect 10). Create cell C1 with formula =B1+1 (expect 11). Evaluate C1 and assert result equals 11. Change A1 to 6 and re-evaluate C1, asserting it produces 13 (via B1=12). Test deeper nesting (4+ levels: A1 → B1 → C1 → D1 → E1). Verify that the engine does not evaluate in arbitrary order; use intermediate assertions to confirm topological correctness.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test is sufficient; no module boundaries or I/O required.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency and returns a circular reference error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is in-process graph logic. A mock cycle detector would always pass and hide real infinite-loop bugs. The real failure boundary is whether the actual evaluation algorithm detects the cycle. A Unit test must trigger real formula evaluation and observe the error response (not a timeout or stack overflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create cell A1 with formula =B1. Create cell B1 with formula =A1. Attempt to evaluate A1 (or trigger workbook recalculation). Assert that a circular-reference error is returned immediately, not a stack overflow or infinite loop timeout. Test multi-step cycles (A1→B1→C1→A1). Test that non-circular chains (A1→B1→A2) don't incorrectly trigger as circular. Add time-out assertion to ensure no actual looping occurs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test directly exercises the cycle-detection code path. No mock can safely replace this.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value, and returns an error when the key is not found
behaviors.justification: VLOOKUP is a function implementation: array search, comparison, and value extraction. All logic is in-process with no external dependencies or module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Create a lookup table (3 columns: product ID, name, price; 5 rows of data). Test VLOOKUP("ID003", table, 2) returns the correct name. Test VLOOKUP("ID999", table, 2) returns a not-found error. Test VLOOKUP with column index out of bounds (expect error). Test VLOOKUP with exact-match mode and approximate-match mode (if applicable). Test case sensitivity (e.g., does VLOOKUP("id003", ...) match "ID003"?).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test fully covers function behavior; no integration or I/O needed.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions
behaviors.justification: Conditional branching is in-process logic. IF is a function that evaluates conditions and returns one of two branches. No external dependencies or module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1=5, A2=15, A3=25. Test =IF(A1>10, "yes", "no") returns "no". Test =IF(A2>10, IF(A2>20, "high", "medium"), "low") with A2=15 returns "medium". Test deeply nested IFs (5+ levels). Test IF with formula results as conditions (=IF(SUM(A1:A3)>40, ...)). If the engine short-circuits (doesn't evaluate unused branches), verify this behavior through side effects or explicit testing.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test is sufficient for this function behavior.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them
behaviors.justification: String concatenation is in-process logic. The implementation must correctly join strings and skip empty cells. No external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1="Hello", A2="", A3="World". Test CONCATENATE(A1, A2, A3) returns "HelloWorld" (empty cell skipped, not inserted). Test CONCATENATE with separator if supported (e.g., CONCATENATE(A1, "-", A3) returns "Hello-World"). Test with numeric values and cells containing formulas. Test with all-empty inputs (expect empty result or special handling). Test with very long strings to verify no truncation or buffer overflow.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test covers the function logic end-to-end.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet references require the formula parser and evaluator to interact with a sheet lookup/registry system. Mocking sheet access could hide bugs in reference parsing (Sheet2!A1), sheet lookup by name, or sheet-not-found error handling. Integration tests with real sheet objects verify the wiring between the parser, sheet registry, and evaluator.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2. Set Sheet2!A1=10. In Sheet1, create a cell with formula =Sheet2!A1+1. Evaluate and assert result equals 11. Test referencing a non-existent sheet (expect error). Test with nested cross-sheet references (Sheet1 references Sheet2, which references Sheet3). Test that changing Sheet2!A1 and triggering recalculation updates dependent formulas in Sheet1. Verify sheet name case sensitivity and special characters in sheet names.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test required to exercise sheet lookup and reference resolution across module boundaries.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order
behaviors.justification: Topological recalculation is a core in-process algorithm. The real failure boundary is whether dependents are recalculated in correct order (dependencies before dependents), not whether a mock topological sort passes. Unit test exercises the actual recalculation algorithm by setting up dependent formulas, changing a source cell, and asserting that all dependents have correct values in the right sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a chain: A1=1 → B1=A1*2 → C1=B1+10 → D1=C1*3. Verify D1 evaluates to 36. Change A1 to 2 and trigger recalculation. Assert D1 re-evaluates to 39 (via B1=4, C1=14, D1=42). Test with multiple branches (A1 has both B1 and C1 as dependents; both update correctly). Test that circular dependencies don't corrupt the topological sort (expect error). Verify order by instrumenting the evaluator (if possible) to log evaluation sequence.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test fully exercises the topological recalculation algorithm.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: Undo/redo involves state management (saving snapshots or deltas) that must interact correctly with the formula engine's internal state. Mocking either the undo system or the engine could hide state corruption, synchronization bugs, or forgotten dependencies. Integration tests verify that the undo module and engine work together to correctly restore formula text and all dependent values.
behaviors.test_description: Create cells A1=1, B1=A1+1 (expect 2), C1=B1*2 (expect 4). Edit B1's formula to =A1*10 (C1 updates to 20). Call undo. Verify B1's formula reverts to =A1+1 and C1 reverts to 4. Call redo and verify B1=A1*10 and C1=20 again. Test undo after multiple sequential edits. Test undo with cell deletions or insertions. Verify that floating-point values don't lose precision during undo/redo cycles.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test required to verify state management and engine synchronization.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas with no data loss or floating-point precision change
behaviors.justification: Saving to disk and loading requires real file I/O and interaction with the OS filesystem. Mocking file operations would hide real bugs: serialization format bugs, floating-point precision loss during binary encoding/decoding, file I/O errors (permissions, disk space, path issues), and endianness/platform-specific issues. Only System tests with actual disk writes/reads can catch these failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with: A1=10, B1=A1*2 (20), C1=B1+5 (25), D1=1.123456789, E1=D1*PI. Add various data types (dates, times, booleans, multi-line strings). Save to a temporary file. Load the file into a new workbook instance. Verify all cell values match exactly (assert floating-point values with sufficient precision; use equality within epsilon if needed). Verify all formulas are preserved (B1 still contains =A1*2, not the value 20). Test with large workbooks (1000+ cells, many formulas). Test with corrupted/truncated files (expect appropriate error, not silent data loss). Test on different filesystems or with file permissions restrictions if applicable.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System test required; mocking file I/O would hide real serialization/deserialization bugs and OS-level failures.
